steps.title: Use perpendicularity of tangent and radius
steps.content: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. This means their slopes multiply to give -1. The radius connects the center $(3, 7)$ to the tangent point $(a, -4)$, and the tangent has slope $\frac{5}{4}$.

steps.title: Write the radius slope in terms of a
steps.content:
Using the slope formula between $(3, 7)$ and $(a, -4)$:

$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-4 - 7}{a - 3} = \frac{-11}{a - 3}$$

steps.title: Apply perpendicularity and solve for a
steps.content:
$$\frac{-11}{a - 3} \cdot \frac{5}{4} = -1$$

$$\frac{-55}{4(a-3)} = -1$$

Multiply both sides by $4(a-3)$:

$$-55 = -4(a - 3)$$

$$-55 = -4a + 12$$

$$-4a = -67$$

$$a = \frac{67}{4}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
